a:r.a:t: Seller Voice Summary: Key Themes from Open Feedback
“I am happy to be here”
“I work on a team with great leadership and guidance.”
“I absolutely love my position and working for HGV... This company has changed my life”
Strong Pride in HGV and Positive Team Experience
Employees express strong pride in HGV and positive sentiment toward their teams and leadership
“HGV sometimes feels that we are broke all the time... it seems that most of Q4, we are always on a lockdown with spending.”
“It is a struggle to get the correct budgeted roles... a lot of nickel and diming.”
“After reading the Proxy Statements... it seems that we had a great year... but that does not translate down.”
Employees perceive a disconnect between company performance and investment in frontline roles and compensation
“Leadership in our area consider us very average and have cut our pay so many times in the last 2 years”
“The compensation plan for pre-call inhouse marketing has become 100 percent unfair and unjust.”
“Compensation is lacking for the industry and does not align with the effort”
Compensation is viewed as inequitable by some, with concerns around reduced earnings and misalignment with effort
Compensation Sometimes Perceived as Unfair and Declining
“Target levels need to be realistic and achievable for midline producers with stretch goals for top producers”
“Leaders are penalized by having very difficult targets the next year for doing well the year before and budgets are not equitable.”
“Targets are set to high”
Some r
espondents
explicitly call out target-setting as unrealistic, inequitable, or overly difficult to achieve.
“If I call 100 people a day Mon-Fri and nobody answers…it isn’t my fault…why am I punished for it?”
“I am grateful for what I earn. However, I do not agree with how I’m compensated, nor do I have total control over it.”
“The success of my compensation is more based on another departments production than my own.”
Performance and compensation are perceived to be driven by factors outside individual control, weakening the link between effort and reward.
“On average, I receive weekly drop of about 13 leads. I would have to book a very high percentage to even hit level 3 or 40 tours.”
“It is not possible for us here at The Point at Poipu to reach higher levels with our current leads.”
“If tours be more high quality that would be great!”
Respondents point to low lead volume, current lead quality, and tour quality as direct barriers to achieving higher performance levels.
Concerns Around Earnings and Financial Structure
Quotas and Targets Seen as Misaligned
Lead Quality and Opportunity Allocation Are Key Pain Points
Misalignment Between Effort, Inputs & Outcomes
Voice of the Employee Survey
